summary: 1 Samuel narrates the transition from judges to monarchy through Samuel’s prophetic ministry, Saul’s rise and fall, and David’s anointing.
historical_context: The book marks a major institutional shift in Israel’s life. It examines kingship under covenant, contrasting outward appearance with inward character and showing the tension between divine rule and human political desire.

mirror_water_fire.mirror: The book reveals the danger of preferring visible power over covenant faithfulness.
mirror_water_fire.water: Prophetic correction washes away illusions of self-made rule.
mirror_water_fire.fire: David’s anointing signals transformation toward a deeper covenant kingship.
